page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU configuration provides 16 physical cores with 1 thread per core under a shared allocation model. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a 40 MB L3 cache. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance dichotomy between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is limited, with stress-ng single-core and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the bottom 10%, and single-threaded gzip compression in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core utilization leverages the 16 physical cores to achieve average tier results in stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks fall within the average range, though the overall PassMark memory score is weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

The ecs.mn4.4xlarge represents an older hardware generation with a shared CPU allocation, which introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs. This profile makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that require high resource density and multi-threaded capacity without needing cutting-edge single-core speeds or local NVMe storage. It is suited for workloads such as static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and moderate database operations, which align with its average multi-core and database benchmark results. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or intensive machine learning workloads, as demonstrated by its poor performance on medium-sized language model inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with a KVM hypervisor, this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model with 16 physical cores and no hyperthreading. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate weak single-core execution, with stress-ng single-core and bzip3 single-threaded decompression placing in the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-threaded benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests, reach average tiers. This instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense, multi-threaded workloads that do not require high single-core performance. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, Redis operations, and moderate database tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 CPU. It offers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core capabilities.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, suited for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance offering sixteen physical cores and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor with 16 physical cores and 64.0 GB RAM
Shared CPU allocation model on x86_64 architecture with KVM hypervisor
Average multi-core performance contrasted by weak single-core benchmark results
No local storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps
Suited for static web serving, Redis operations, and moderate database workloads
